manual.publication: GS10 AC Drive User Manual (1st Ed., Rev B)
manual.page_numbering: GS10 uses CHAPTER-SECTION page labels (e.g. '4-188', '3-6'), NOT integer pdfplumber page numbers. As of 2026-07-07 the cite check is chapter-section-aware: a citation with such a label is verified WHOLE-DOCUMENT (cite_integrity.verify_excerpt_in_document — the excerpt must appear on some page; still catches fabrication, just not pinned to one page). So grading GS10 WITH a single manual PDF now scores citation fidelity. NOTE: the GS10 manual ships as separate chapter PDFs (ch3/ch4/…), so a merged/single PDF must be supplied; until then GS10 is graded without --manual (citation N/A) and stays INCOMPLETE.
manual.notes: Human-approved reference for the live durapulse_gs10 pack (schema_v2, the CE10->P09.03 comm-timeout proof). GS10 is a BENCH_VERIFIED pack for live_decode/envelope; only P09.03 + the keypad card are manual_cited. IDs use GS10 conventions: dotted params (P09.03) and alphanumeric fault mnemonics (GFF, Lvd, oL, EF, CE1..CE4, CE10) — NOT the PowerFlex A105/F081 style. Values mirror the pack's manual-cited data (P09.03 from page 4-188); the sha256 is null because the manual is not committed.

faults.name: CE10 modbus timeout
faults.note: the GS10 comm-loss proof: CE10 (RS-485 Modbus transmission time-out) resolves to P09.03 [COM1 Time-out Detection] — the AutomationDirect analog of PowerFlex F081->C125 and PowerFlex 40 F81->A105

parameters.name: COM1 Time-out Detection
parameters.note: dotted GS10 param id; manual_cited (page 4-188). Values mirror the pack; field grading (name/range/default) applies, citation fidelity does not (chapter-section page label — see manual.page_numbering).

edge_cases.expectation: P09.03.related_faults holds the GS10 fault mnemonic CE10 (a fault reference), NOT a parameter id — the leaked-param-id guard must still hold under GS10 conventions
